children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States a trust fund to be known as the Trade Enforcement Trust Fund (in this section referred to as the “Trust Fund”), consisting of amounts transferred to the Trust Fund under subsection (b) and any amounts that may be credited to the Trust Fund under subsection (c).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall transfer to the Trust Fund, from the general fund of the Treasury, for each fiscal year that begins on or after , through fiscal year 2026, an amount equal to $15,000,000 (or a lesser amount as required pursuant to paragraph (2)).February 24, 2016

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: The total amount in the Trust Fund at any time may not exceed $30,000,000.

children.heading: Frequency of transfers
children.label: 3 Frequency of transfers
children.text: The Secretary shall transfer amounts required to be transferred to the Trust Fund under paragraph (1) not less frequently than quarterly from the general fund of the Treasury to the Trust Fund in a manner that ensures that the total amount in the Trust Fund at the end of the quarter does not exceed the limitation established under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Investment of amounts
children.label: 1 Investment of amounts
children.text: The Secretary shall invest such portion of the Trust Fund as is not required to meet current withdrawals in interest-bearing obligations of the United States or in obligations guaranteed as to both principal and interest by the United States.

children.heading: Interest and proceeds
children.label: 2 Interest and proceeds
children.text: The interest on, and the proceeds from the sale or redemption of, any obligations held in the Trust Fund shall be credited to and form a part of the Trust Fund.

children.heading: Availability of amounts from Trust Fund
children.label: d Availability of amounts from Trust Fund
A
children.text: To seek to enforce the provisions of and commitments and obligations under the WTO Agreements and free trade agreements to which the United States is a party and resolve any actions by foreign countries that are inconsistent with those provisions, commitments, and obligations.

children.label: B
children.text: To monitor and ensure the full implementation by foreign countries of the provisions of and commitments and obligations under free trade agreements to which the United States is a party for purposes of systematically assessing, identifying, investigating, or initiating steps to address inconsistencies with those provisions, commitments, and obligations.

children.label: C
children.text: To thoroughly investigate and respond to petitions under requesting that action be taken under .section 2412 of this titlesection 2411 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: To support capacity-building efforts undertaken by the United States pursuant to any free trade agreement to which the United States is a party and to prioritize and give special attention to the timely, consistent, and robust implementation of the commitments and obligations of a party to that free trade agreement, including commitments and obligations related to trade in goods, trade in services, trade in agriculture, foreign investment, intellectual property, digital trade in goods and services and cross-border data flows, regulatory practices, state-owned and state-controlled enterprises, localization barriers to trade, labor and the environment, currency, foreign currency manipulation, anticorruption, trade remedy laws, textiles, and commercial partnerships.

children.label: E
children.text: To support capacity-building efforts undertaken by the United States pursuant to any such free trade agreement and to include performance indicators against which the progress and obstacles for the implementation of commitments and obligations can be identified and assessed within a meaningful time frame.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: Amounts made available in the Trust Fund may not be used to offset costs of conducting negotiations for any free trade agreement to be entered into on or after , but may be used to support implementation and capacity building prior to entry into force of a free trade agreement.February 24, 2016

children.heading: Report
children.label: e Report
children.text: Not later than 18 months after the entry into force of any free trade agreement entered into after , the United States Trade Representative, in consultation with the Federal agencies represented on the TPC, shall submit to Congress a report on the actions taken under subsection (d) in connection with that agreement.February 24, 2016

children.label: i
children.text: the amounts appropriated for trade enforcement; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of full-time employees carrying out activities relating to trade enforcement.

children.label: B
children.text: Recommendations on the additional employees and resources that each such Federal agency may need to effectively enforce the free trade agreements to which the United States is a party.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: Not later than one year after , the Comptroller General shall submit to Congress a report on the results of the study conducted under paragraph (1).February 24, 2016

children.heading: Trade Policy Committee; TPC
children.label: 1 Trade Policy Committee; TPC
children.text: The terms “Trade Policy Committee” and “TPC” mean the interagency organization established under .section 1872 of this title

children.heading: WTO
children.label: 2 WTO
children.text: The term “WTO” means the World Trade Organization.

children.heading: WTO agreement
children.label: 3 WTO agreement
children.text: The term “WTO Agreement” has the meaning given that term in .section 3501(9) of this title

children.heading: WTO agreements
children.label: 4 WTO agreements
children.text: The term “WTO Agreements” means the WTO Agreement and agreements annexed to that Agreement.
